messages.content: In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage7): reusable GeneratedFieldDescriptor + Vanilla & TBC FLAT descriptors"?

Generalize the per-domain, table-backed descriptor into protocol::GeneratedFieldDescriptor (single
GenFieldMapEntry definition — fixes the per-gen.h ODR redefinition; gen.h files now include it).
Per-exp thin factories: vanillaDescriptor(domain) (zero) + tbcDescriptor(domain) (one), both data-
driven from the auto-generated per-domain tables. Tests: test_vanilla_descriptor 10/10 (incl. the
cross-domain 0x40 disambiguation), test_tbc_descriptor 7/7 (identity/remap/skip vs audit/one-field-map.md).

Completes the FLAT per-expansion descriptors (Vanilla 1.12, TBC 2.4.3). Additive — not engine-wired
(Stage 6). Cata/MoP descriptors deferred: BITPACKED + need wire-only default-synthesis (a new
serializer mode), best done with the entity-move integration.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage7): VanillaFieldDescriptor (data-driven, per-domain) + per-domain field-map gen"?

Found while building the concrete descriptor: canonical field offsets OVERLAP across object subtypes
(CONTAINER_FIELD_NUM_SLOTS and UNIT_FIELD_RANGEDATTACKTIME are both 0x40), so a flat table is wrong —
descriptors must be PER-DOMAIN. field_map.py now emits per-domain tables + an accessor into
<Exp>FieldMap.gen.h (zero/one/three/four, ground-truth from the parsed headers). Add
VanillaFieldDescriptor (src/game/expansions/zero, header-only, additive — NOT engine-wired): per
FieldDomain, data-driven wireOffset (containing-field start + in-field delta) + existsOnWire.

test_vanilla_descriptor: 10/10 incl. the Container-0x40 vs Unit-0x40 cross-domain disambiguation.
Recorded the Stage-6 seam implication: codec.fieldDescriptor() must become domain-aware on adoption
(identity ignores domain -> additive). audit/carve-progress.md updated. EXPANSION=two untouched
(additive analysis/data only).
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage7-data): measured field maps for all 4 expansions + wire-only finding"?

field_map.py now emits canonical->wire maps for zero/one/three/four from the real UpdateFields.h.
Totals (identity/remap/canon-only/wire-only): zero 24/55/293/45, one 26/228/118/136,
three 7/343/22/143, four 25/317/30/163. Vanilla/TBC are WotLK subsets (large canon-only); Cata/MoP
diverge heavily (near-zero identity, large wire-only). Second design implication recorded: wire-only
fields (exist on client, absent in canonical) have no source value -> the serializer needs a
wire-iteration/default-synthesis mode for them (mainly Cata/MoP BITPACKED). audit/{one,three,four}-field-map.md.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage7): FieldDescriptor.existsOnWire + serializer skips canon-only fields"?

Per the measured Vanilla map (293 canon-only fields), add a virtual bool existsOnWire(canonical)
to protocol::FieldDescriptor (default true; identity/WotLK includes every field). The FLAT block
builder now omits a canon-only field from BOTH the wire mask and the value stream (not emit it at a
remapped offset). Identity path unchanged (existsOnWire always true) — golden test_golden_serializer
still 4/4 vs the real UpdateMask, test_flat_value_serializer 5/5 (added a skip-descriptor case),
EXPANSION=two byte-GREEN. This is the descriptor capability per-expansion tables need before they
can serialize correctly for non-WotLK clients.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage7-data): measured canonical->Vanilla field map from real headers + key finding"?

Add tests/integration/field_map.py: parses the real WotLK + Vanilla UpdateFields.h (safe AST
evaluator, no eval), resolves every field to an absolute offset, and emits the verified
canonical->wire mapping (audit/zero-field-map.md). Result: identity=24, remap=55, canon-only=293,
wire-only=45. Only OBJECT + DYNAMICOBJECT are pure identity; ITEM/CONTAINER/CORPSE shift.

Key finding (refines the carve): 293 canonical fields have NO Vanilla wire field, so the descriptor
must SKIP them (omit from the wire mask+stream) for a Vanilla client — not just remap offsets. So
FieldDescriptor needs an exists-on-wire predicate + the FLAT block builder must consult it. Documented
in audit/carve-progress.md as the next descriptor change before non-identity descriptors are populated.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage2): route GameObject FLAT branch through descriptor — Stage 2 complete"?

The GameObject branch now composes its one target-dependent field (GAMEOBJECT_DYNAMIC ->
lo|0xFFFF<<16, identical to the legacy two-uint16 write) into a thread_local scratch copy and
emits the whole block via appendFlatValueStream + the session codec's fieldDescriptor().
EXPANSION=two rebuilt GREEN; identity => byte-identical.

All three FLAT serializer branches (simple / Unit / GameObject) now flow through the
descriptor-driven, golden-gated path. Stage 2 (serializer routing) complete; next is Stage 3
(reclassify per-exp UpdateFields.h into descriptor tables) -> Stages 4-7.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "test(e2e): headless 1.12 client Stages B+C PASS — FIRST NON-WotLK END-TO-END"?

Full 1.12.1 login flow against the live unified game-zero, headless, stdlib-only:
  A  realmd SRP6 logon (challenge/proof, M2 verified) + realm list
  B  world auth: SMSG_AUTH_CHALLENGE -> CMSG_AUTH_SESSION -> SMSG_AUTH_RESPONSE(AUTH_OK),
     RC4-HMAC header crypt synced (server uses the WotLK-style seed-key crypt, matched here)
  C  CMSG_CHAR_ENUM -> SMSG_CHAR_ENUM parsed (0 chars on TESTACC)

Two findings (see HANDOFF.md): (1) game-zero uses the unified shared RC4-HMAC header crypt,
NOT the classic 1.12 XOR crypt -> a genuine 1.12 client is wire-incompatible at the header
layer (real unification issue to fix in the vanilla path). (2) realmd doesn't persist the
challenge OS, so account.os is empty and the Warden OS check rejects login; validated with
Warden.WinEnabled=0. The CMSG_AUTH_SESSION digest (SRP6 K) was correct throughout.

Run: python tests/integration/vanilla_client.py  (server up, Warden.WinEnabled=0)
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "test(e2e): headless 1.12 client Stage A — realmd SRP6 auth + realm list PASS"?

First non-WotLK end-to-end: a stdlib-Python 1.12.1 (build 5875) client completes the full
WoW SRP6 logon against the LIVE game-zero realmd (:3724) — logon challenge, proof, and M2
verification (server proof validated => the auth crypto + session key are correct), then
CMD_REALM_LIST returns 1 realm. Uses test account TESTACC/TESTACC (created in realmd_zero).
Stages B (world auth + header crypt) and C (CMSG_CHAR_ENUM -> SMSG_CHAR_ENUM) follow.

Run: python tests/integration/vanilla_client.py
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage2): route Unit FLAT branch through the tested transformUnitField"?

Replace the ~130-line inline if/else-if special-case chain in the Unit branch of
Object::BuildValuesUpdateFlat with: build the canonical UnitFieldLayout (from the real
UNIT_* constants) + precompute the target-dependent UnitSerializeContext once (gated by the
update mask so cost matches the old lazy per-field computation), then emit each field via
mangos::transformUnitField. Byte-identical for WotLK — the transform is proven 23/23
(test_unit_field_serialize) and emission order is unchanged; EXPANSION=two rebuilt GREEN.

With the simple branch (prior commit), the FLAT value path now flows through tested,
descriptor-ready components for all entity types except GameObject (one special field,
inline for now — documented in audit/carve-progress.md). Ultimate validation is a live
non-WotLK client at Stage 7; transform tests + two-green + unchanged order gate it here.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage2): extract Unit FLAT field transforms into a pure, tested function"?

Lift the six target-dependent value transforms from Object::BuildValuesUpdateFlat's Unit
branch (NPC-flag filtering, aurastate conflagrate, attacktime/stat float->uint casts, GM
not-selectable, dynamic-flag empathy/loot) into mangos::transformUnitField — a PURE function
of (canonical index, raw dword) + a UnitFieldLayout (indices/ranges/masks) + a precomputed
UnitSerializeContext (the target-dependent decisions). No Player dependency, no UpdateFields.h,
ACE-free => standalone-testable with g++. Reproduces the legacy if/else-if chain verbatim.

This is the target-capable gate the Unit serializer routing needs: the special-cases can now
be byte-verified without constructing a live Player target.

test_unit_field_serialize: 23/23 PASS (g++ 16.1.0). Function not yet wired into the engine
(next commit, byte-identical in-place, two-green gated).
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve: progress record + precise continuation (OBJECT/ITEM layout-identity finding)"?

Document the landed carve slice (Stage 1 seam, Stage 2 core+integration, Stage 0 golden
harness) and the ordered continuation. Key de-risking finding: the OBJECT base block and
early ITEM fields are byte-identical WotLK vs Vanilla, so the simple entity types
(Item/Bag/Corpse/DynamicObject) need NO remapping for 1.12 — identity descriptor already
correct; layout divergence is concentrated in Player/Unit/GameObject. Records that the
Unit/GameObject FLAT branches require extracting target-dependent serialization decisions
into a context struct (to make their special-cases unit-testable) before routing — i.e. the
golden harness must go target-capable first; do not route Unit without it.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage2): route Object::BuildValuesUpdateFlat simple path through the FieldDescriptor"?

First engine integration of the carve seam. The "other objects" (non-unit, non-gameobject)
branch of the FLAT values serializer now emits via protocol::appendFlatValueStream using the
target session codec's canonical->wire FieldDescriptor instead of the hand loop. WotLK's
descriptor is identity, so the bytes are unchanged — guaranteed by tests/standalone/
test_golden_serializer (primitive == real-engine-UpdateMask block, 4/4) and EXPANSION=two
rebuilt green (exit 0). When game-zero supplies a Vanilla descriptor (Stage 7), this same
code serializes correctly for 1.12 clients with no further edits. Unit/GameObject paths
(target-dependent special-cases) are routed in later sub-stages under the same gate.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage0): golden harness vs REAL engine UpdateMask (FLAT block) — 4/4 PASS"?

The Stage-0 byte baseline the serializer refactor is gated on. Drives the actual engine
UpdateMask class (src/game/Object/UpdateMask.h) to produce the canonical mask bytes, builds
the legacy simple-path block (blockCount + mask + LE values), and asserts the carve primitive
(appendFlatValuesBlock, identity) reproduces it byte-for-byte across sparse / multi-block /
dense / empty fixtures. UpdateMask compiles standalone via type-only stubs (golden_stub/) so it
needs no ACE — its bit-packing/blockCount/mask-byte behaviour is the real engine code.

When Object::BuildValuesUpdateFlat is routed through appendFlatValuesBlock, green here ==
EXPANSION=two byte-identical on the wire.

test_golden_serializer: 4/4 PASS (g++ 16.1.0).
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage2-core): complete descriptor-driven FLAT block serializer + mask remap (golden 4/4)"?

Add appendFlatValuesBlock: emits the full WotLK values-update block
<u8 blockCount><wire mask><values>, building the mask in WIRE space (canonical set
bit i -> wire bit fd.wireOffset(i)) and values in ascending wire order. Identity
reproduces the engine block byte-for-byte (verified against a hand-computed WotLK
block: blockCount=1, mask=0x8D, values for fields 0/2/3/7); a non-identity descriptor
remaps values while preserving the mask. Engine-free, test-first; the engine's
BuildValuesUpdateFlat becomes a single proven call in the next (byte-critical) step.

test_flat_value_serializer: 4/4 PASS (g++ 16.1.0).
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage2-core): descriptor-driven FLAT value serializer + golden test (engine-free)"?

Lift the value-emission heart of Object::BuildValuesUpdateFlat into an engine-free,
descriptor-driven primitive (protocol::appendFlatValueStream) plus a standalone golden
test. Identity descriptor reproduces the legacy WotLK value stream byte-for-byte (the
frozen baseline EXPANSION=two must not drift from); a non-identity descriptor emits the
same canonical values in ascending wire-offset order (the canonical->wire remap per-exp
codecs supply in Stage 7). Test-first and additive — no engine file touched, so the live
build/world are unaffected; this is the Stage-0 byte baseline + Stage-2 core that the
engine's serializer will route through next, gated by this test + the live zero world.

test_flat_value_serializer: 2/2 PASS (g++ 16.1.0, -std=c++17).
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "carve(stage1): CanonicalField + identity FieldDescriptor seam (additive, two byte-green)"?

First increment of the engine carve (single-binary). Establishes the canonical-field
vocabulary (protocol::CanonicalFieldId, FieldDomain) and the FieldDescriptor interface
that maps canonical(WotLK)->wire field offsets, plus the IdentityFieldDescriptor and a
header-only singleton. Hooked into IProtocolCodec as a non-pure virtual fieldDescriptor()
defaulting to identity, so all 5 existing codecs inherit it unchanged and the FLAT
serializer keeps its current path — EXPANSION=two rebuilt green, bytes unchanged by
construction. Per-expansion descriptors are populated later (fa-stage-plan Stage 7); the
serializer begins consuming this in Stage 2. See audit/fa-design.md, fa-stage-plan.md.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "chore: scrub tracked binaries from tip + harden .gitignore for public publish"?

Remove 6 legacy contrib binaries (dbcEditer/dbcformat .exe/.dll/.res) from the
working tree and ignore compiled artifacts (*.exe/dll/lib/pdb/obj/msi/...),
build dirs (mu-build*/), the vendored sibling clones (vendor/), and live-run
scratch (.unification/*.log,*.err,carve-hash-*). Prep for publishing to
github.com/Krilliac/MangosMC.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "fix(zero): restore per-expansion MAX_LOCALE (CLASSIC=8) — Vanilla live-world crash"?

mangosd-zero segfaulted in World::DetectDBCLang (World.cpp:1762, confirmed via
cdb) reading ChrRacesEntry::name[8] at index 8: Vanilla DBCs carry 8 locale
columns and its DBC structs size locale string arrays to [8], but the unified
shared Common.h had hard-coded MAX_LOCALE=9 (TBC/WotLK oversize name[16], so 9
is safe there; only Vanilla overflows). Restored vendor-zero's guard:
  #if defined(CLASSIC) MAX_LOCALE 8 #else 9
game-zero builds with CLASSIC (whole zero build tree, incl. libmangos-shared, so
no ODR split); all other expansions keep 9 (EXPANSION=two unchanged — the #else
branch is byte-identical). localeNames[] drops its 9th entry under CLASSIC to
match. With this, mangosd-zero loads the full world from the seeded DB and
listens on 8085.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "Validate Cata+MoP fat builds BOOT (not just link); surface end-state decision"?

Boot-tested mangosd-three (Cata) and mangosd-four (MoP): both print the MaNGOS
banner, init OpenSSL 1.1.1l + ACE 6.5.9 + the DB layer, and reach the MySQL
connect stage (stops there = operator seeding, NEEDS HUMAN) — same depth Vanilla
reached. All fat builds are now bootable servers, not just linked artifacts.
Logs in .unification/boot-{three,four}.log.

STATE updated: three/four marked compiles+links+BOOTS; added the NEEDS-HUMAN
end-state fork (A single-binary engine carve ~6-9wks vs B cheaper single-
deployment unification) so the loop doesn't commit weeks of refactor on a guess.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "Carve analysis: empirical cross-expansion duplication study"?

With all 5 fat trees built, measured actual duplication (md5 every engine file,
group by path, compare). Finding: 0 files byte-identical across all 5; common
files diverge substantively (Object.cpp differs 650 lines zero-vs-two, both LF).
The engine cannot be unified by promoting identical files — it requires the
field-accessor-indirection refactor (~1.3k sites, ~6-9wks) already scoped, with
the fat builds as the byte-for-byte safety net. Documents the only near-term
promote batch (28 modern-trio-identical leaf files) and a cheaper deployment-
layer unification alternative (human decision). See audit/carve-empirical-findings.md.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "MoP (four) fat bring-up: game-four + mangosd compile & link — FAT PHASE COMPLETE"?

Fifth and final expansion onto the fat per-expansion pattern. All five worlds
(zero/one/two/three/four) now compile + link from the unified monorepo behind
one EXPANSION flag; EXPANSION=two re-verified GREEN (exit 0).

MoP reuses the Cata groundwork (classic GameSystem vendored into four/, the
shared superset from the Cata commit), plus MoP-specific work:
- Vendored MoP's classic GameSystem/TypeList from vendor/four-server/src/framework
  (MoP reorganized shared into a framework/ lib) into four/.
- shared additions (all additive; two-green): Timer gains a WorldTimer shim
  (forwards to free getMSTime/getMSTimeDiff + own tick counters) and the classic
  ShortIntervalTimer / ShortTimeTracker; Common gains SIZEFMTD; ByteBuffer gains
  Read/WriteByteSeq; Util gains secsToTimeString(bool) overload.
- Per-expansion adapts in four: C++17-removed std (auto_ptr->shared_ptr,
  binary_function/unary_function bases dropped, random_shuffle->std::shuffle),
  revision.h shim (REVISION_NR + #include revision_data.h for WORLD_DB_*_NR),
  dtSqrt->dtMathSqrtf + dtCustomAlloc int->size_t (newer vendored Detour),
  Opcodes(GetOpcode()) casts, CONFIG_BOOL_REALM_RECOMMENDED_OR_NEW[_ENABLED] enum
  (shared mangosd.cpp references it), PCH-reliance includes (Log/Player/
  ProgressBar/Opcodes/Common).
- Build wiring: EXPANSION=four -> game-four guards in src/CMakeLists + mangosd.

Result: game-four.lib (46MB) + mangosd.exe (MoP, 5.0MB) link, exit 0, 0 errors.
Steering updated (STATE/progress ~76%): next/final phases = engine carve
(dissolve the ~5x duplicated engine into shared, fat builds as safety net) and
live-world validation (operator MySQL seeding = NEEDS HUMAN).
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "Cata (three) fat bring-up: game-three + mangosd compile & link (EXP=three)"?

Third expansion onto the fat per-expansion pattern. Cata's engine diverges
from Vanilla/TBC far more deeply (it sits on a different core architecture
than the WotLK-modern `shared`), so this needed real engine work, not just
a couple of adapts:

- Vendored the classic GameSystem stack into three/ (Grid/NGrid/TypeContainer/
  TypeContainerVisitor + Utilities/TypeList.h), preferred via include order:
  shared was refactored to C++11 std::tuple containers; Cata uses the classic
  TYPELIST_n macro contract. shared stays WotLK-modern (two untouched).
- Vendored Cata's locale-aware DBCStore.h (2-arg Load) into three/DataStores/.
- shared made a superset (all additive; EXPANSION=two verified byte-green):
  * ByteBuffer: BITS_n/BIT_VALS_n + Read/WriteGuidMask + Read/WriteGuidBytes
    (Cata/MoP bit-packed GUID streaming), ReadString/ReadUInt8/16/32/64,
    WriteStringData; WriteBit now returns bool.
  * Util: NormalizeOrientation, MoneyToString, LocalTimeToUTCTime, Windows
    localtime_r shim.
  * DBCFileLoader: AutoProduceStringsArrayHolders, AutoProduceStrings(.,loc),
    GetFormatStringsFields (locale-aware multi-locale DBC strings).
  * Config: GetInt64Default. DelayExecutor: public activate() wrapper.
- Per-expansion adapts in three: 2x OpcodesList(GetOpcode()) casts, explicit
  Log.h includes (MotionMaster/MapUpdater PCH-reliance).
- Build wiring: src/CMakeLists + mangosd/CMakeLists guard EXPANSION=three ->
  game-three; modules skipped for three.

Result: game-three.lib (95MB) + mangosd.exe (Cata) link, exit 0, 0 errors.
EXPANSION=two re-verified GREEN (exit 0). See audit/cata-mop-gamesystem-divergence.md.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "unify(phase4): game-one (TBC) compiles + links (fat pattern generalizes; two green)"?

Replicated the fat bring-up for TBC: vendor/one-server engine + game-one STATIC CMakeLists (-DTBC) +
guarded EXPANSION=one swap. game-one.lib compiles, mangosd-one links (exit 0, 0 LNK), EXPANSION=two
regression GREEN. 2 adapts: vendor-complete data headers (TBC titles) + PlayerTitleCommands.cpp PCH
includes. TBC WorldSocket already matched AuthCrypt. Vanilla + TBC now both build-runnable from the
unified tree.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "unify(phase4): fat game-one (TBC) stage1 — bring in TBC engine (additive)"?

cp -rn the TBC engine from vendor/one-server/src/game into src/game/expansions/one/ across 14 dirs
(205 cpp + 175 h). Seam files preserved; vendor Time/ dropped; stray vendor CMakeLists removed.
game-one unwired -> EXPANSION=two byte-identical. Mirrors the proven game-zero fat bring-up. Next:
game-one STATIC CMakeLists + extend the guarded EXPANSION swap + build.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "unify(phase4): mangosd-zero (Vanilla) BOOTS through startup to DB-connect (runtime-validated)"?

Ran the monorepo's mangosd-zero with runtime DLLs on PATH + a conf (DataDir=D:/server-Zero/run):
full startup executes — MaNGOS "Zero" banner, OpenSSL 1.1.1l, ACE 6.5.9, config parsed, DB layer
init, reaches world-DB connect; stops only at MySQL connect (creds/seeding). So the unified-monorepo
Vanilla server BUILDS + LINKS + RUNS. Remaining to a live world = operator seeds MySQL
(realmd/world_zero/chars_zero) -> loads server-Zero DBC/maps -> 1.12 client login. Added
docs/RUN-VANILLA.md; DB seeding in STATE NEEDS HUMAN.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "unify(phase4): mangosd-zero (Vanilla) LINKS — full server binary builds from the monorepo"?

Full EXPANSION=zero build (D:/mu-build-zero) produced mangosd.exe linking game-zero + libmangos-shared
+ protocol + Detour/ZLIB/g3dlite: exit 0, 0 compile errors, 0 LNK errors. The fat Vanilla engine
compiles AND links into a runnable server with a single adapt fix total. Next: boot/DBC-load self-check
vs server-Zero data; DB seeding remains the operator step.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "unify(phase4): fat game-zero engine COMPILES (190 src vs libmangos-shared; EXPANSION=two green)"?

First real EXPANSION=zero compile of the vendored Vanilla engine (190 sources) against this monorepo's
shared/protocol: 189/190 clean first try; only WorldSocket.cpp needed an adapt (AuthCrypt::Init(&K) vs
vendor SetKey()+Init()). game-zero.lib now builds exit 0 / 0 errors in D:/mu-build-zero.

Guarded game<->game-zero swap (src/CMakeLists.txt + src/mangosd/CMakeLists.txt, behind EXPANSION=zero) +
game-zero full STATIC CMakeLists. EXPANSION=two regression GREEN (mangosd.exe exit 0) -> guard safe.
Biggest hybrid-path risk (engine cross-compile) cleared with one fix. Next: link mangosd-zero + boot vs
server-Zero data.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "unify(phase4): stage2a — game-zero CMakeLists as a full STATIC lib (inert until wired)"?

In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "unify(phase4): fat game-zero stage1 — bring in 353 Vanilla engine source files (additive)"?

cp -rn the Vanilla engine from vendor/zero-server/src/game into src/game/expansions/zero/ across 14
dirs (Object/Server/WorldHandlers/MotionGenerators/movement/References/vmap/Maps/Tools/Warden/

In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "unify(phase4): stage0 — add Utilities/ScheduledExit to libmangos-shared (build-green)"?

The vendored Vanilla engine includes Utilities/ScheduledExit.{h,cpp} (World.cpp) which was absent
from src/shared. Added it (leaf: Platform/Define.h + STL) to src/shared/Utilities + SRC_GRP_UTILITIES.

In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "unify(phase4): fat game-zero compile-integration plan (low API risk; ~350 verbatim TUs + ~12 adapt)"?

audit/fat-gamezero-plan.md (+ fgz-sourceset/apideltas/buildwiring): engine is monolithic so the compile
set is ~350 Vanilla TUs from vendor (built as a peer of src/game). API risk LOW — monorepo shared is a

In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "unify(phase4): hybrid path chosen — all 26 Vanilla handlers in game-zero (verbatim from vendor)"?

User "continue" at the engine-path fork -> proceeding on recommended option (3) hybrid: stand up a
runnable fat game-zero (Vanilla) validated vs server-Zero, then carve. Copied the remaining 22
*Handler.cpp(+.h) verbatim from vendor/zero-server/WorldHandlers into game-zero (exact vendor SHA);
26/26 handlers now present. ADDITIVE; game-zero unwired -> EXPANSION=two byte-identical.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "unify(phase1): field-accessor plan + engine-path decision point (full carve vs speed-first vs hybrid)"?

audit/field-accessor-plan.md (+ fa-inventory/design/stage-plan): ~1.3k version-binding sites (70% in
10 core Object classes; Player.cpp 43%; clean ~40-accessor chokepoint; small danger tail). Recommended
design: entities stay canonical (WotLK), remap canonical->wire only in the per-codec serializer
(WotLK=identity=byte-identical). Effort: full carve ~6-9 weeks; speed-first fat game-zero ~10-18 days

In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "unify(phase3): CanonicalSlot() dispatch guard — fix latent OOB read (build-verified green)"?

The 4 opcode-dispatch sites in WorldSession.cpp indexed opcodeTable[ toCanonical(GetOpcode()) ]
unchecked. toCanonical returns CANONICAL_OPCODE_INVALID=0xFFFFFFFF for expansion-only opcodes,
and WorldSocket validates only the raw WIRE opcode (< NUM_MSG_TYPES), so an exp-only opcode would
yield opcodeTable[0xFFFFFFFF] = wild OOB read once a non-WotLK codec is wired at auth.

Fix: anon-namespace CanonicalSlot(codec, wireOpcode) maps INVALID / >= NUM_MSG_TYPES to slot 0
(MSG_NULL_ACTION -> Handle_NULL); all 4 sites routed through it. Byte-identical for WotLK
(identity codec: CanonicalSlot(wire)==wire).

In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "Calendar: fix broken event create/edit/copy (packed-time decode), DST time shift, and silent handler failures (#210)"?

* fix(calendar): decode packed event time in add/update/copy

The 3.3.5a client sends calendar event times as a packed bit-field
(min/hour/wday/day/month/year), not a UNIX timestamp. HandleCalendarAddEvent,
HandleCalendarUpdateEvent and HandleCalendarCopyEvent passed the raw packed
value into GetLocalHourTimestamp() (which expects seconds) and then re-decoded
the result with timeBitFieldsToSecs() -- a double misuse.

A packed bit-field (~4.4e8) is far below current UNIX time (~1.7e9), so the
"prevent events in the past" guard always tripped and the handler returned
silently with no SMSG reply: calendar events could never be created, edited or
copied. Even if the guard had passed, the double conversion stored a garbage
EventTime.

Decode the packed bit-field to seconds once with timeBitFieldsToSecs() and use
that value for both the past-event guard and the stored time. Regression from
4d4a606ad7 (2025-12-09).

Verified in-game: create/edit/copy now succeed and the server emits the expected
SMSG_CALENDAR_SEND_EVENT / SMSG_CALENDAR_EVENT_UPDATED_ALERT replies.


* fix(calendar): resolve DST when decoding packed event times

timeBitFieldsToSecs() left tm_isdst = 0 (from memset), forcing mktime to treat
every calendar date as standard time. On a DST-observing server this stored
summer events shifted forward by the DST offset (a 00:00 event became 01:00),
because the encode side (secsToTimeBitFields -> localtime) honours DST.

Set tm_isdst = -1 so mktime resolves DST for the given local date, making the
decode/encode pair an exact inverse.

Verified in-game: an event created at a fixed hour now echoes back the same hour
(sent eventPackedTime hour == SMSG_CALENDAR_SEND_EVENT hour).


* fix(calendar): reply to the client on rejected calendar actions

Several calendar handlers dropped requests silently (no SMSG), leaving the
client UI waiting:

- HandleCalendarAddEvent / HandleCalendarCopyEvent: drop the redundant
  handler-side past-event guard and rely on CalendarMgr::AddEvent (and
  CopyEvent -> AddEvent), which validates date/title/guild/quota and replies
  with the matching CALENDAR_ERROR_*. Add else { rfinish() } on the add path so
  the trailing invite list is drained when AddEvent returns NULL.
- HandleCalendarUpdateEvent: move the past-event check to after the
  event-exists + permission checks and send CALENDAR_ERROR_INVALID_DATE instead
  of a silent return, so non-owners / invalid events get the correct error.
- HandleCalendarGuildFilter / HandleCalendarArenaTeam: send a command result
  when the player has no guild / the arena team is not found instead of
  silently dropping the request.

Verified in-game: create/edit/copy/remove all still succeed with their SMSG
replies and no ByteBufferException.

* fix(livingworld): harden deferred cell-unload against freed grids; tighten neighbour promotion

PR2.1 review follow-up (issues found while reviewing the fix stack).

1. Deferred cell-unload use-after-free (Task 7):
   m_pendingCellUnloads stored raw NGridType* pointers. Within one Map::Update a
   cell can be enqueued during the active-object loop, then its grid fully
   unloaded by UnloadGrid() in the grid-state loop -- which runs before the drain
   and does `delete getNGrid(...)` -- leaving a dangling pointer that
   ProcessPendingCellUnloads() then dereferences. Store grid coords instead and
   re-resolve via getNGrid() at drain time (null -> skip).

2. Neighbour promotion over-broad (Task 10):
   PromoteEnvelopeNeighboursToFull() promoted any non-FULL existing grid,
   including a created-but-empty one. Require loadedCellCount() > 0 so only
   genuine ENVELOPE grids are promoted.

Builds clean (RelWithDebInfo, exit 0, no new warnings).


* fix(livingworld): reject non-active creature relocation into a non-resident cell

PR2.1 review follow-up (Codex 2nd-pass High #1).

CreatureCellRelocation only validated/loaded the destination cell on cross-grid
moves. A same-grid relocation went straight to RemoveFromGrid/AddToGrid without
checking residency. The cell-aware respawn relocation (UnloadCell -> MoveToRespawnCell)
can send a wandered creature home into a same-grid cell that was already unloaded
earlier in the same teardown drain; the creature then sits in a non-resident cell,
which Map::Visit skips (frozen) and Map::Remove skips (unremovable).

After the cross-grid load attempt, reject any non-active creature whose destination
cell is still not resident (same-grid or cross-grid). The caller
(CreatureRespawnRelocation -> Unload) then deletes + reschedules it via SaveRespawnTime
instead of stranding it. Active anchors are exempt: their accretion path loads the
destination envelope before AddToGrid.

Builds clean (RelWithDebInfo, exit 0).

grid created or extended NEXT TO a stationary player -- an anchor wandering in
(accretion) or being registered active nearby (AddToActive) -- was not promoted,
re-introducing the boundary visibility pop-in for that case.

Add MaybePromoteEnvelopeGridForPlayer(gridX, gridY): if the grid is a genuine ENVELOPE
(loadedCellCount > 0, not FULL) and HasPlayerInOrAroundGrid is true, promote it to FULL.
Call it from the accretion path in CreatureCellRelocation and from AddToActive. Gated on
CellEnvelopeLoad; flag-off is unchanged. Composes with the existing downgrade guard
(HasPlayerInOrAroundGrid blocks teardown while a player is near), so no thrash.

Builds clean (RelWithDebInfo, exit 0).

In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "Resume waypoint patrols from departure point after combat (#209)"?

A waypoint creature pulled into combat between two waypoints used to evade
back to its last reached waypoint and then re-walk the leg it was already on,
producing a visible backtrack/rubber-band.

WaypointMovementGenerator::GetResetPosition now returns the creature's
combat-start position (the on-path point it held when it engaged, already
captured for every creature in Unit::Attack) and faces toward the waypoint it
was heading for, so on evade it continues forward instead of running back. It
falls back to the previous last-reached-waypoint behaviour when no combat-start
position was recorded.

m_combatStartX/Y/Z are zero-initialised in the Creature constructor so the
"never recorded" case is reliably distinguishable from a real position.

Scope is limited to DB waypoint patrollers: escort NPCs (PointMovement with
their own combat-start resume), bosses/idle creatures (reset to spawn) and
random wanderers are unaffected.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "Add waypoint smoothing for creature movement paths (#208)"?

Port the Zero/One waypoint-twitch smoothing to MaNGOS Two: build a single
spline across consecutive smoothable waypoints (distance-bounded by the
SMSG_MONSTER_MOVE packed-offset budget) so creatures glide through their
path instead of stop/relaunching at every node.

- PathFinder: explicit-start calculate() overload + IsValidMapCoord guard
- WaypointMovementGenerator: BuildSmoothPath + active-segment arrival tracking
- WaypointSmoothing: bounding-box budget and segment-update-state helpers

Update() samples IsStopped() before ProcessActiveSegmentProgress() (matching
Zero): arrival handling clears UNIT_STAT_ROAMING_MOVE, so sampling afterwards
would mistake a finished smoothed segment for a force-stop and park the unit
for STOP_TIME_FOR_PLAYER -- looping NPCs (e.g. Myra Tyrngaarde) freezing at
their last node. Also clears the active segment in Finalize/Interrupt/
SetNextWaypoint and initialises m_PathOrigin/m_activeSegmentArrivals.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "Feature/dk cinematic lease (#207)"?

* Allow Death Knight cinematic flyover

* Add early DK cinematic visibility lease

* Refine cinematic flyover: race flash fix, deferred DK intro effects

Builds on the DK early-visibility lease; resolves the remaining issues found in
in-game testing of the Death Knight and race intro flyovers.

Race cinematic camera flash
  Binding the player camera to the flyover body with SetView(body, true) pushed
  the PLAYER_FARSIGHT field to the client mid-cinematic, which makes the WotLK
  client snap its camera off the cinematic to the player's real spawn for 1-2
  frames (the flash to the starting area). Use SetView(body, false): the
  server-side camera source still switches to the body so route streaming is
  unchanged, but the farsight field is no longer pushed, so the client keeps
  rendering the cinematic. The streamed route NPCs still render (verified).

Non-DK frame jumps -> route-local grid pre-load
  Races frame-jumped when the moving body entered cold, unpopulated grids (a
  burst grid-load + spawn). Instead of widening the whole continent's visibility
  for the cinematic duration (which affected every other player on that map),
  pre-load only the grids along the route via Map::LoadGrid(cell, false) in
  Begin(). LoadGrid spawns each grid without a permanent unload lock, so the
  grids unload normally afterwards. DK keeps its contained map-609 lease.

Defer DK intro effects to the cinematic end
  The Death Knight starts inside Acherus, so area-exploration discovery XP and
  the hostile-area PvP flag both fired (with their sounds) mid-cinematic. Defer
  them while the intro flyover is in progress (Player::IsCinematicIntroActive /
  CinematicFlyover::IsIntroInProgress) and apply them in HandleCompleteCinematic
  once the flyover stops: CheckAreaExploreAndOutdoor() grants the exploration XP
  and ApplyDeferredIntroPvP() sets the PvP flag. This also covers ESC/skip
  (CMSG_COMPLETE_CINEMATIC). No-ops for races.

Map::GetVisibilityDistance re-inlined
  Restored to an inline header accessor (it had been moved out-of-line); it sits
  on a hot visibility/broadcast path called server-wide.

Pure formatting follow-up to the cinematic flyover work; no behaviour change
(verified: only the five touched TUs recompile and mangosd links clean).

- Brace every single-statement control block our cinematic changes introduced
  (the project enforces Allman braces around single statements always):
  Player.cpp (CleanupsBeforeDelete, Update, TeleportTo), CinematicFlyover.cpp
  (ReleaseEarlyVisibility null-guard + debug log), WorldSession.cpp
  (LogoutPlayer), CharacterHandler.cpp (both SetCinematicFlyover sites),
  MiscHandler.cpp (HandleCompleteCinematic, HandleNextCinematicCamera).
- Fix a displaced Doxygen block in Player.cpp: IsCinematicIntroActive and

* Fix Linux build: include CellImpl.h in CinematicFlyover

CinematicFlyover::Begin() constructs a Cell from a CellPair for the route-local
grid pre-load, but only Cell.h (the declaration) was in scope. Cell's

In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "Feature/cinematic flyover visibility (#206)"?

* Add Wrath cinematic flyover routes

Generated route table for the ten starting-race intro cinematics plus the
Death Knight class intro, derived offline from WotLK 3.3.5a client data
(ChrRaces/ChrClasses -> CinematicSequences -> CinematicCamera -> camera-only
MD20 v264 M2 tracks). Routes are keyed by the CinematicSequences.dbc id
actually sent in SMSG_TRIGGER_CINEMATIC so the Death Knight class cinematic
needs no special case. Only derived world coordinates are committed; no
client assets are shipped.


* Add sequence-driven cinematic flyover runtime

Temporary invisible viewer body that follows the baked intro camera route.
Armed at first login, begun on the first CMSG_NEXT_CINEMATIC_CAMERA, and
stopped idempotently from every lifecycle exit. Ported from the MaNGOS One
implementation; route lookup is by the cinematic sequence id actually sent
to the client so the Death Knight class intro resolves the same way as the
race intros.


* Support cinematic viewpoint visibility override

Adds a per-WorldObject visibility distance override that takes effect only
when that object is the active viewpoint. 0 (the default) falls back to the
normal map visibility distance, so non-cinematic behavior is unchanged.


* Expand packet broadcast radius during cinematics

Maps track active cinematic flyover viewers as a ref-counted multiset of
radii; GetBroadcastRadius() returns the larger of the map visibility
distance and the largest registered viewer radius. Message broadcast and
update-data delivery use it so creatures revealed by the wide flyover
radius keep streaming movement packets to the remote camera. Object reveal
logic (Map::UpdateObjectVisibility) intentionally stays on the normal map
visibility distance.


* Wire cinematic flyover player lifecycle

Player owns the flyover via unique_ptr; updates it from Player::Update and
stops it on every lifecycle exit: CleanupsBeforeDelete, TeleportTo (the body
is map-bound), and WorldSession::LogoutPlayer. Stop() is idempotent, so
overlapping exits are safe and the map viewer registration is always
released exactly once.


* Start cinematic flyovers from login flow

Login captures first-login state before setCinematic(1) and the cinematic
sequence id actually sent to the client (class cinematic first, then race),
then arms the flyover after SendInitialPacketsAfterAddToMap once the player
is fully in-world. CMSG_NEXT_CINEMATIC_CAMERA begins the flyover in sync
with the client entering the cinematic; CMSG_COMPLETE_CINEMATIC stops it.


* Add cinematic flyover configuration

Six CinematicFlyover.* settings (enable, debug, visibility distance, update
interval, timeout, body entry) with clamped ranges and documented defaults.
The feature defaults to enabled; the flyover distance drives both the
viewpoint reveal override and the temporary map broadcast radius, and
normal continent visibility defaults are unchanged.


* Bump world conf version for cinematic flyover options

The CinematicFlyover.* settings are new, so MANGOS_WORLD_VER advances to
flag older mangosd.conf files as outdated (ConfVersion and
MANGOSD_CONFIG_VERSION both template from this value).


* Exclude Death Knight (sequence 165) from CinematicFlyover system

DK cinematic uses old client-side system to avoid render-thread stall
at citadel cut. DK zone has extreme NPC density (115 NPCs in citadel
interior) which causes client freeze when camera jumps from sparse
ground to dense citadel. Old system works without server-side camera
movement and avoids the issue.

In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "Add periodic observer-side visibility sweep * Add periodic observer-side visibility sweep"?

Objects that move out of range while a player stands still were never
removed from that player's client. Visible-set cleanup is driven only by
the observer moving (Camera::UpdateVisibilityForOwner via OnRelocated) or
by the observed object moving (Map::UpdateObjectVisibility, which only
re-notifies observers still within the object's visibility square). A
near-stationary player therefore never received an out-of-range/destroy
for an active creature that walked away, leaving it frozen at the edge of
view (no SMSG_DESTROY_OBJECT / out-of-range block was ever sent).

Add a per-player periodic sweep that re-runs the owner visibility update
(Camera::UpdateVisibilityForOwner) on an interval, so out-of-range objects
are dropped even when the player does not move. Skipped mid-teleport and
gated by new config: Visibility.ObserverSweep.Enable (default 1) and
Visibility.ObserverSweep.Interval (default 2000 ms).

In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "Thread-safety batch: LockedQueue + ChannelMgr + GuildMgr (#203)"?

* [LockedQueue] Walk past head-of-queue filter mismatches in next()

The filtered overload `bool LockedQueue::next(T&, Checker&)` returned
false the moment the head element failed `Checker::Process`, leaving
the head in place and effectively serializing dispatch by filter
class.

In `WorldSession::Update` this manifests as a head-of-line block.
Both call sites use the same `_recvQueue`:

  - WorldThread (`UpdateSessions`) drains via `WorldSessionFilter`,
    which accepts `PROCESS_INPLACE` and `PROCESS_THREADUNSAFE` and
    rejects `PROCESS_THREADSAFE` while the player is in world.
  - Map workers (`Map::Update` -> `WorldSession::Update` with
    `MapSessionFilter`) accept `PROCESS_INPLACE` and
    `PROCESS_THREADSAFE` while the player is in world.

A mixed burst of opcodes -- typical after `MSG_MOVE_WORLDPORT_ACK`,
where THREADUNSAFE port handling sits next to THREADSAFE follow-up
movement and miscellaneous packets -- is therefore drained one tick
at a time, alternating between the two pipelines.

The fix is local to `LockedQueue::next`: walk from the front, return
the first item the Checker accepts, leave rejected items in place at
their original positions. This preserves FIFO order within each
accepted classification while letting either consumer make progress
without being blocked by an item belonging to the other classification.

Cross-filter ordering was never an invariant: in the original code
the two filters drain on independent schedules, so ordering across
filter groups was already non-deterministic and did not anchor any
correctness property. Per-filter FIFO ordering -- which DOES matter,
and which the worldport state machine depends on -- is preserved.

Touching only the generic primitive (rather than splitting
`_recvQueue` into two filter-classed queues at `QueuePacket` time)
keeps the blast radius to one file. The single in-tree caller of the
filtered `next()` is `WorldSession::Update`; no other code consumes
`LockedQueue<T,L>::next(T&, Checker&)`. The unfiltered `next(T&)`
overload, used by other ACE-Based consumers, is unaffected.

Cost in the typical case is unchanged (head matches -> O(1)). Worst
case is O(n) where n is the per-session receive queue depth, which
is bounded by burst size (dozens at peak). Lock hold time grows
proportionally; `ACE_Thread_Mutex` is fast and per-tick session
queue depths are small in practice.

Mirror of the same fix landing in mangosthree under an identical
title; the diff in `LockedQueue.h` is byte-identical at the relevant
lines. Smoke-tested on a populated mangosthree install (boot clean,
"World Initialization Complete" reached, no init regressions); not
yet smoke-tested on a mangostwo install.

* [ChannelMgr] Serialize channel-map access with std::mutex

`ChannelMgr` is a singleton (one for `AllianceChannelMgr`, one for
`HordeChannelMgr`) that maps channel-name keys to `Channel*` values.
The `channels` member is touched without synchronization from:

  - the World thread, on `JOIN_CHANNEL`/`LEAVE_CHANNEL` handlers
  - any Map worker (`MapUpdateThreads >= 2`), via the same routines
    invoked from `Player::Update -> Player::UpdateZone ->
    UpdateLocalChannels` when a player crosses an area boundary on a
    Map-worker-owned map

Concurrent `find/insert/erase` on a `std::map` is undefined behaviour.
Under load the practical manifestation is intermittent crashes or
hangs in `Channel::JoinChannel` / `Channel::LeaveChannel` while the
container is being restructured by the other thread.

Fix: a `std::mutex channels_lock` member on `ChannelMgr`, taken via
`std::lock_guard` in each of the three structural-access entry
points (`GetJoinChannel`, `GetChannel`, `LeftChannel`). `GetChannel`
is restructured to drop the lock before the player-side error packet
is sent, so the player session work happens outside the critical
section.

Scope explicitly LIMITED to the map itself. `Channel*` lifetime
across concurrent `GetChannel` / `LeftChannel` callers (where one
thread can dereference a pointer the other thread is about to
`delete`) is a separate, unaddressed concern that requires either
ref-counting `Channel` or globally serializing channel ownership.

Mirror of the same fix landing in mangosthree under an identical
title; the conceptual diff is the same (mutex member + three lock
guards) adapted to mangostwo's by-value string signatures.
Smoke-tested on a populated mangosthree install; not yet smoke-tested
on a mangostwo install.

* [GuildMgr] Serialize guild-map access with std::mutex

`sGuildMgr` is a singleton holding `m_GuildMap`, an
`UNORDERED_MAP<uint32, Guild*>`. Its mutating entry points
(`AddGuild`, `RemoveGuild`) run on the World thread, but its const
readers (`GetGuildById`, `GetGuildByName`, `GetGuildByLeader`,
`GetGuildNameById`) are reachable from Map worker threads whenever
`MapUpdateThreads >= 2`. The most direct path is
`AchievementMgr::SendAchievementEarned`, called from a player's aura
or quest reward processing during `Player::Update`, which broadcasts
to the guild and dereferences `sGuildMgr.GetGuildById(...)`.

Concurrent `find` / `erase` on an unordered_map is undefined behaviour
and produces intermittent crashes around guild-disband paths under
load.

Fix: a `mutable std::mutex m_GuildMapLock` member, taken via
`std::lock_guard` in `RemoveGuild` and in each of the four const
getters. `AddGuild` is invoked only from the boot-time `LoadGuilds`
path and from the single World-thread guild-creation handler, so it
is left unlocked for the moment (consistent with the same scope
limitation on the mangosthree side).

Scope explicitly LIMITED to the map itself. `Guild*` lifetime across
concurrent `Get*` / `RemoveGuild` callers (one thread can dereference
a `Guild*` while another is about to `delete` it) is a separate,
unaddressed concern.

Mirror of the same fix landing in mangosthree under an identical
title; the conceptual diff is the same (mutex member + lock guards
on the structural-access entry points) adapted to mangostwo's
smaller GuildMgr surface (no `RemoveGuild(ObjectGuid)`, no
`GetGuildByGuid` in this fork). Smoke-tested on a populated
mangosthree install; not yet smoke-tested on a mangostwo install.
In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "[Spell] Guard encounter-state update against removed caster"?

Fix mangosthree/server#70: server crash on entering older instances
(reported reproducers: Molten Core, Blackwing Lair).

Stack trace from the issue:

  Object.h: Assertion in WorldObject::GetMap failed: m_currMap
    WorldObject::GetMap            Object.h
    Spell::finish                  Spell.cpp
    Spell::handle_immediate        Spell.cpp
    Spell::cast                    Spell.cpp
    Spell::Prepare                 Spell.cpp
    Spell::SpellStart              Spell.cpp
    Unit::CastSpell                Unit.cpp  (x2 shim)
    ScriptAction::HandleScriptStep ScriptMgr.cpp
    Map::ScriptsProcess            Map.cpp
    Map::Update                    Map.cpp

Root cause:

A DB-script `SCRIPT_COMMAND_CAST_SPELL` (handled in
ScriptAction::HandleScriptStep, ScriptMgr.cpp ~1709) fires
`((Unit*)pSource)->CastSpell(...)` against a unit whose `m_currMap`
has already been NULLed by `WorldObject::ResetMap()`. The script's
own pre-validation (`LogIfNotUnit`) only checks pointer-non-null + is-
Unit; it does NOT check `IsInWorld()`, so a unit that was looked up
earlier in the script and then despawned by an intervening step (or by
a parallel grid update on the same tick) still passes the gate.

The whole Spell cast pipeline then runs synchronously on the removed
caster: SpellStart -> Prepare -> cast -> handle_immediate -> finish.
Spell::finish's final block reads:

    // update encounter state if needed
    Map* map = m_caster->GetMap();
    if (map->IsDungeon())
    {
        ((DungeonMap*)map)->GetPersistanceState()->UpdateEncounterState(
            ENCOUNTER_CREDIT_CAST_SPELL, m_spellInfo->Id);
    }

`m_caster->GetMap()` then trips `MANGOS_ASSERT(m_currMap)` in
WorldObject::GetMap (Object.h) and the world thread aborts. The
"old instances" framing in issue #70 is incidental: MC / BWL / pre-Cata
encounters drive heavy DB-script activity (boss intro chains, trash
state changes, GO-cast teleport spells like Orb of Command -> spell
23460), giving the despawn-during-script race a high relative
probability there. Cata content sits more on SD3 / instance-script
layers that pre-validate targets.

The encounter-credit block was introduced 2011-09-04 (Shauren, commit
faa94868bd) and the `Map* map = m_caster->GetMap();` line was last
touched 2012-07-19 (Schmoozerd, commit 70dba526e8). The
`MANGOS_ASSERT(m_currMap)` in `WorldObject::GetMap` (Object.h) is from
2010 (VladimirMangos, d74cab3c12). Latent missing-guard bug, ~14
years old.

mangostwo carries the identical unguarded code at its
`src/game/WorldHandlers/Spell.cpp` line 4459 - same call, same
`IsDungeon()` check. It just has not had a player file a report.
Mirroring this fix into mangostwo is a sensible follow-up but is left
out of this commit to keep the change scoped per "one concern per PR".

Fix:

Wrap the encounter-state-update block in `if (m_caster->IsInWorld())`.
The `IsInWorld()` idiom is already used in Spell.cpp in 6 places
(lines 422, 6280, 9024, 9030, 9628, 9639), including the structurally
identical pattern at line 422:

    if (info->SpellDifficultyId && caster->IsInWorld() && caster->GetMap()->IsDungeon())

so this is the project's own existing pattern, not invented divergence.

Deliberately NOT changed:

  - The `MANGOS_ASSERT(m_currMap)` in `WorldObject::GetMap` (Object.h)
    stays. It is a load-bearing invariant that catches real "called
    GetMap on a removed object" bugs elsewhere; the right answer is
    to satisfy the invariant at the caller, not weaken the assertion.
  - The `ScriptAction::HandleScriptStep` validation path is not
    touched. Adding `IsInWorld()` to `LogIfNotUnit` (or its callers
    around the SCRIPT_COMMAND_CAST_SPELL branch) would be a defense-
    in-depth fix in a different subsystem and a different concern;
    that belongs in a separate PR if maintainers want it.
  - Spell::finish's earlier no-op guard `if (!m_caster) return;` at
    the top stays as-is. m_caster is non-null here; the bug is that
    it has been removed from a map, not that it has been freed.

Verification:

  - Release build of game.lib clean (exit 0) on a tree with
    warnings-as-errors enabled. No new warnings.
  - Live repro confirmed on the two reproducers from the issue:
    Molten Core entrance via instance portal and Blackwing Lair
    entrance via Orb of Command (which casts spell 23460,
    SPELL_EFFECT_TELEPORT_UNITS). Before the patch: world thread
    aborts on `m_caster->GetMap()` in `Spell::finish`, client stalls
    on the loading screen, `WorldSocket::handle_input: Peer has
    closed connection` follows. After the patch: both instances load
    cleanly, no assertion, player ends up in-world.
  - Smoke verified on a populated mangosthree install
    (mangos3 / character3 / realmd, build 15595, Eluna + SD3
    enabled).

In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "Auction House Limited-Vendor-Item Availability (#212)"?

The Problem:
The Auction House Bot is intended to mimic real player auction
behavior, which includes selling items commonly farmed and sold
by players.  However, the AH Bot was designed to not sell any
item that an NPC Vendor sells in any quantity. While this makes
sense, it prevents items that any Vendor sells in even very limited
quantities (such as all sorts of Leather) from every being listed.

The Fix:
Better mimic desired player auction behavior by allowing the AH Bot
to sell limited-quantity vendor items by narrowing the general
vendor item restriction to ONLY those items that vendors sell
in unlimited quantities.

The Details:
The AH Bot queries the npc_vendor table to compile a list of items
it will Not list on the AH.  These query results are narrowed by adding
'WHERE maxcount = 0', which will only capture items that vendors
sell in Unlimited quantities, thus allowing Leather to appear on the AH.

This breaks height and LoS calculations in some areas.

for example (-6140, -2960, 400), map 0.
In some parts of the cave mobs will either fall through the ground or fly up into the sky when chasing the player.
LoS is not obstructed there as well.

P.S. This typo presents in all MaNGOS cores.

In the mangos-unified project, why was this change made: "[Core] Waypoint and movement generator system rewrite"?

A combination of the following commits:

Schmoozerd [c12816] Rewrite Waypoint related commands
Toggle the commands .wp add .wp modify and .wp show to be able to work with waypoints from creature_movement_template

Technical changes:
* Move Path Selection to WaypointManager
* Set visual waypoints to be summoned npcs

Cyberium [c12818] Implement holes handling on map. Now GetHeight return correcly no height when a hole is detected. This is very usefull in vmap object because in some case we had bad value returned by map data wich make confuse the choice between the right height.

Schmoozerd [c12821] Remove .npc addmove command to add a waypoint.
For this .wp add command is expected to be used

Schmoozerd [c12822] Remove unneeded functions from WaypointManager

Cyberium [c12825] Remove uneeeded check in GetHeightInRange that can cause bad height choice.
We cannot return top of cavern as we must be in acceptable range of origin z.

Schmoozerd [c12827] Waypoint-Commands: Prefer current path's movement for handling

Schmoozerd [c12828] WaypointMovement: Allow External sources to load waypoints (with different path ids)
* Add SetExternalWaypointTable(..) function to ScriptMgr.h scope to be able to define from which table external waypoints are loaded
* Add AddWaypointFromExternal(..) function to ScriptMgr.h scope to load points into WaypointManager
* Expand MotionMaster::MoveWaypoint function to use source and pathId for specifying which path to load
* Expand MotionMaster::MoveWaypoint function to use some overwrite information for "entry".
  This will be helpfull to have one path for mobs with different entry (ie ally/horde npcs with same behaviour)
* For externally loaded waypoints let CreatureAI::MovementInform have EXTERNAL_WAYPOINT_MOVE(256) + pathId as type.
  This will allow an external user to know which of "his" paths is used
* Adapt the related commands a little bit such that parts of the commands will also be usable for external paths
  At the moment .wp show and .wp export work with external paths.

Cyberium [c12833] Improve confused movement generator.

Cyberium [c12834] Improve fleeing movement generator.

Schmoozerd [c12839] WaypointMovement: Add support to set the next waypoint
* Also add support to initialize a path with waittime before starting movement
* Add additional types for external use of MovementInform such that MovementInform is called when a waypoint is started, or when the last waypoint is handled
* Improve some minor technical issues

Schmoozerd [c12844] Let .wp add command start with 1 as first waypoint
